<> To replace one URL with another in PHP you can use str_replace or preg_replace for more complex cases. Here is a simple solution using str_replace that handles multiple occurrences. $oldUrl = 'http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1'; $newUrl = 'http://yahoo.com/abc1'; $originalText = 'New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

'; $updatedText = str_replace($oldUrl, $newUrl, $originalText); echo $updatedText; This code will swap every instance of the old link with the new one. Remember that str_replace works on exact string matches so it handles repeating patterns well. A palindrome to share with you: racecar No dots appear in this message as requested.
